item.title: Creamy Brown Butter Mashed Potatoes to Wow Your Guests
item.description: Creamy brown butter mashed potatoes that are super easy to make! With rich, nutty flavors and a silky texture, these spuds will be a hit at any dinner table. Serve them at your next gathering and see the smiles! Pin this recipe for later!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 pounds Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and quartered
  • 1/2 cup Kerrygold butter, browned
  • 1/2 cup whole milk, warmed
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • Fresh chives for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. First, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Toss in the quartered potatoes and let them simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until tender. You wanna check with a fork – they should fall apart easily.
  2. While the potatoes are cooking, melt the Kerrygold butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Keep an eye on it and stir occasionally until it turns a beautiful golden brown and smells nutty. Don’t walk away – it can go from brown to burnt in a flash!
  3. Once the potatoes are done, drain them and toss them back in the pot. This step helps to steam off any excess moisture. It’s all about that creamy texture, you know?
  4. Now, pour in the brown butter and warmed milk. Mash everything together until you get a smooth and creamy consistency. Add salt and pepper to taste, but trust your instinct here – you know what you like!
  5. Serve them hot, garnished with fresh chives. The contrast of the green against the creamy potatoes is just... well, you’ll see!

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229818868 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #yukon #kerrygold #whole

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Creamy Brown Butter Mashed Potatoes to Wow Your Guests
media:content.media:description: Creamy brown butter mashed potatoes that are super easy to make! With rich, nutty flavors and a silky texture, these spuds will be a hit at any dinner table. Serve them at your next gathering and see the smiles! Pin this recipe for later!

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761229818868, Easypost, 1hourmeals, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, yukon, kerrygold, whole

item.title: Master Southern Thanksgiving Sides Like a Pro
item.description: Get ready to impress with these Southern Thanksgiving sides! From rich turkey gravy to flavorful cornbread dressing and perfectly caramelized sweet potatoes, each dish brings comfort to the table. You’ll wanna save this recipe for your next family feast!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 30 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 60 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 cups cornbread, crumbled (I use Bob's Red Mill mix for the best texture)
  • 2 cups vegetable broth, low-sodium
  • 1 cup onion, finely chopped
  • 1 cup celery, finely chopped
  • 1 teaspoon sage, dried (or fresh if you can find it)
  • 1 teaspoon thyme, dried
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 2 tablespoons Kerrygold butter
  • 3 cups sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 1/4 cup brown sugar
  • 1/4 cup maple syrup
  • 2 cups turkey gravy, homemade or store-bought

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Alright, first things first - preheat your oven to 350°F. While that’s heating up, let’s get your cornbread ready. If you’re using Bob's Red Mill, prepare it according to the package instructions. You want it slightly dry.
  2. Next, grab a skillet and melt the Kerrygold butter over medium heat. Toss in your chopped onion and celery. Sauté for about 5-7 minutes until they’re soft and fragrant. Seriously, the smell is gonna make your kitchen feel like home!
  3. Now, in a large bowl, combine the crumbled cornbread, sautéed veggies, sage, thyme, and enough vegetable broth to moisten everything. You want it to be moist but not soggy. Add salt and pepper to taste. It should feel like a warm hug.
  4. Spread that mixture into a greased baking dish and cover with foil. Bake for 25 minutes. Then remove the foil and bake for another 15-20 minutes until it gets a nice golden crust. That crunch on top? So good.
  5. While that’s baking, let’s tackle the sweet potatoes. Boil them in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash with brown sugar and maple syrup. You want them smooth but with a few chunks for texture. Trust me, this combo is a game changer!
  6. For the gravy, just heat it up in a saucepan until bubbling. Now, serve everything hot and watch it disappear faster than leftovers at a family gathering.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229818868 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #cornbread #vegetable #onion

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: Get ready to impress with these Southern Thanksgiving sides! From rich turkey gravy to flavorful cornbread dressing and perfectly caramelized sweet potatoes, each dish brings comfort to the table. You’ll wanna save this recipe for your next family feast!

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761229818868, Mediumpost, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, cornbread, vegetable, onion
